tags.description: This building is a modern homage (built 1983) of the demolished Bogardus Building/Edgar Laing Stores (1849-1971). It's neither an exact replica nor at the same location, but was reconstructed here with modern materials.

This lighthouse is a memorial to the passengers, officers and crew who died as heroes when the steamship Titanic sank after collision with an iceberg. Lattitude 41° 46' north, Longitude 50° 14' west, April 15, 1912.
